idea-plugin.name: Javelin - An SBFL Tool for IntelliJ
idea-plugin.description:
Javelin -- Spectrum-Based Fault Localization for Java

Javelin automates Spectrum-Based Fault Localization (SBFL) for Java projects. It instruments your JUnit tests, builds a coverage spectrum, and ranks every source line or method by suspiciousness -- so you can find the bug instead of searching for it.

Key Features

  • One-click analysis -- run from the Tools menu or press Ctrl+Shift+J
  • 4-tier visual highlighting -- red / orange / yellow / green bands in the editor, gutter, and scrollbar
  • Results table -- sortable, filterable, with double-click navigation to source
  • Export to CSV -- share or archive ranked results with percentile and band classification
  • Status-bar readiness widget -- instant project health check before analysis
  • Run configurations -- save and reuse custom analysis settings
  • Auto-detect paths -- automatically detects target, test, and source directories for Gradle and Maven projects
  • Persistent settings -- save defaults that persist across sessions via Settings > Tools > Javelin

Algorithm Support

  • Ochiai (default) -- standard SBFL algorithm using coverage spectrum
  • Ochiai-MS (experimental) -- mutation-aware variant that integrates PITest mutation analysis for enhanced fault localization

Output Options

  • Granularity: Statement-level (default, ranks individual lines) or method-level (aggregates to methods using max score)
  • Ranking: Dense (default, recommended for debugging) or average/MID (for SBFL evaluation and EXAM scores)
  • Offline mode: Pre-instruments bytecode to avoid Java agent conflicts with Mockito-inline, ByteBuddy, PowerMock, JMockit, or AspectJ

Requirements

  • IntelliJ IDEA 2025.1 -- 2025.3.x (Community or Ultimate)
  • Java 21+ JDK configured in the project
  • A Java project with JUnit tests (at least one failing test required)

Documentation: GitHub README

action.description: Run fault localization on the current project
Clear highlights, gutter icons, stripe marks and result table
